name: CFSC Checks Cashed 76th & Mill Rd
description_short: CFSC at 76th & Mill Rd offers check cashing without bank account requirements, plus money transfers, bill pay, and prepaid debit cards with extended hours daily.
description_long:
CFSC Checks Cashed 76th & Mill Rd is a community financial services location in Milwaukee, Wisconsin specializing in alternative financial services for unbanked and underbanked consumers. The location has operated as part of the larger CFSC network, which provides accessible financial services to customers who may not have traditional bank accounts or prefer not to use them.

The Milwaukee location at 6404B N 76th St offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including check cashing (personal and business), money orders, electronic bill pay, money transfers (including Western Union), prepaid debit cards (Yes! Debit Account), ATM access, foreign currency exchange for 80+ countries, tax refund services, transit/MetroCard purchases, and Bitcoin cryptocurrency services. They also provide phone trade-in services through EcoATM kiosks and prepaid cellular services. Operating hours are Monday-Friday 7am-9pm, Saturday 8am-7pm, and Sunday 9:30am-5pm.

This location distinguishes itself through extended operating hours (7am opening on weekdays, open Sundays), no credit check requirements for prepaid debit accounts, and no bank account requirement for check cashing. The company emphasizes friendly service and convenience for customers handling routine financial tasks like paycheck cashing, family money transfers, and bill payments. They operate multiple Milwaukee locations and are actively hiring.

The primary caveat is that while specific fees are not disclosed on the website, check-cashing services typically charge percentage-based fees (2-5% of check value is common in the industry). Customers should call (414) 875-6607 to confirm current fee structures and service availability before visiting. The location's suitability depends on whether the convenience of extended hours and no-account-required services justifies their fee-based pricing model compared to traditional banking.
pros: No bank account required to cash checks — only photo ID and personal contact information needed
Extended weekday hours (7am-9pm Mon-Fri) enable access before and after typical work schedules
Open on Sundays (9:30am-5pm), unlike many traditional banks
No credit check required for Yes! Debit Account prepaid cards
Accepts multiple forms of government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, state ID, student/work ID)
Instant check clearing — no waiting period like traditional banks
Comprehensive service suite: 15+ services including money transfers, bill pay, foreign currency, and Bitcoin at a single location
Affiliated with Western Union, enabling worldwide money transfer capability
cons: Specific fee structure and check-cashing percentages not disclosed on website; requires phone call to confirm pricing
Prepaid debit cards require ongoing account maintenance and may carry monthly fees not detailed on site
Foreign currency exchange and specialized services (Bitcoin, tax refunds) may not be available at all CFSC locations despite being listed
Limited information about customer protections, dispute resolution, or service guarantees on the location page
Website emphasizes convenience but does not transparently compare total costs versus traditional bank checking accounts
best_for: Unbanked individuals needing check cashing without opening a bank account
Shift workers requiring evening/weekend financial services access
People sending money internationally who need Western Union or other transfer services
Customers seeking a no-credit-check prepaid debit card alternative
diagnosis: CFSC 76th & Mill Rd is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need immediate check cashing without account requirements and value extended evening/weekend hours over traditional banking. The primary caveat is that fees are not transparently disclosed online, so customers must call ahead to compare total costs against standard bank checking accounts before committing to services.
